rationale: The citing paper uses Zauberman et al. (2009) to motivate a core theoretical step in Study 3: perceived prospective duration affects intertemporal discounting. This is not just a literature-list mention; the citation directly supports a prediction that one-year versus 12-month framing should change discount rates when relevance is high. The cited work therefore plays a meaningful conceptual role in hypothesis setup and interpretation of that study.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Zauberman et al.'s subjective-time mechanism for discounting to its own framing question, predicting and testing frame-driven differences in discount rates. It uses that prior account as the basis for showing how personal relevance moderates this effect.
